title: Orca
summary: Orca is one of Solana's most recognizable trading surfaces. Arcium treats it as an important DeFi counterpart for encrypted markets and has published a dedicated article exploring private on-chain trading with Orca.
description:
Orca describes itself as a homepage for buying crypto on Solana, covering swaps, pools, and token discovery. That makes it a meaningful reference point for how encrypted routing, hidden intent, or confidential execution could matter in live Solana market structure.

Arcium has gone beyond a generic ecosystem listing here: it published a dedicated piece with Orca on exploring encrypted on-chain markets and also includes Orca in its DeFi ecosystem coverage. I still did not find a current Orca product page that documents a shipped Arcium-powered feature, so Atlas should keep the classification at ecosystem project rather than confirmed integration.

bodySections.title: Why_It_Matters
bodySections.body: Orca sits close to core Solana order flow through swaps and pools. For Atlas, that makes it useful not just as a partner name on a list, but as a concrete example of where encrypted market structure could intersect with mainstream user behavior.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium published a dedicated article with Orca on encrypted on-chain markets and also includes Orca in the encrypted DeFi ecosystem. That is stronger than a generic partner mention, but still stops short of a currently documented Orca-side Arcium feature.

statusNote: Arcium has both listed Orca in its DeFi ecosystem and published a dedicated market-structure article with Orca, but I did not find a current Orca product page describing a live Arcium-powered module.

sources.label: Orca Website
sources.note: Official site for Orca's Solana trading and liquidity surfaces.

sources.label: Arcium x Orca
sources.note: Arcium's dedicated article exploring encrypted on-chain markets with Orca.
